2605.09287.title: PiCA: Pivot-Based Credit Assignment for Search Agentic Reinforcement Learning
2605.09287.paper_summary: Large Language Model (LLM)-based search agents trained with reinforcement learning (RL) have significantly improved the performance of knowledge-intensive tasks. However, existing methods encounter critical challenges in long-horizon credit assignment: (i) Reward Sparsity, where models receive only outcome feedback without step-level guidance to differentiate action quality; (ii) Isolated Credit, where credit is assigned to steps independently, failing to capture sequential dependencies; and (iii) Distributional Shift, where rewards are estimated on templates that deviate from the model's natural generative distribution. To address these issues, we propose Pivot-Based Credit Assignment (PiCA), a novel step reward mechanism that reformulates the search trajectory as a sequential process of cumulative search progress. Unlike prior isolated step rewards, PiCA defines process rewards as success probabilities dependent on the historical context based on Potential-Based Reward Shaping (PBRS). This approach identifies pivot steps, which comprise target golden sub-queries and sub-answers derived from historical trajectories, as information peaks that significantly boost the likelihood of a correct final answer. By anchoring these step rewards to the final task objective, PiCA provides dense, pivot-aware and trajectory-dependent guidance while maintaining distributional consistency. Extensive experiments show that PiCA outperforms existing strong baselines across seven knowledge-intensive QA benchmarks, achieving 15.2% and 2.2% improvements for 3B and 7B models. The consistent performance gains across various models show PiCA's robust generalization. The code is available at https://github.com/novdream/PiCA.
2605.09287.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）基於強化學習（RL）訓練的搜索代理在知識密集型任務的表現上有了顯著提升。<lb/>然而，現有方法在長期信用分配上面臨關鍵挑戰： (i) 獎勵稀疏性，模型僅獲得結果反饋，而沒有逐步指導來區分行動質量；<lb/>(ii) 隔離信用，信用獨立地分配給每一步，未能捕捉序列依賴性；<lb/>(iii) 分佈轉移，獎勵是在偏離模型自然生成分佈的模板上進行估算的。<lb/>為了解決這些問題，我們提出了基於樞紐的信用分配（PiCA），這是一種新穎的步驟獎勵機制，將搜索軌跡重新定義為累積搜索進展的序列過程。<lb/>與先前的孤立步驟獎勵不同，PiCA 將過程獎勵定義為依賴於基於潛力的獎勵塑造（PBRS）的歷史背景的成功概率。<lb/>這種方法識別樞紐步驟，這些步驟由來自歷史軌跡的目標金色子查詢和子答案組成，作為顯著提高正確最終答案可能性的資訊高峰。<lb/>通過將這些步驟獎勵與最終任務目標相連接，PiCA 提供了密集的、關注樞紐的和依賴於軌跡的指導，同時保持分佈一致性。<lb/>大量實驗表明，PiCA 在七個知識密集型 QA 基準上超越了現有的強基準，對於 3B 和 7B 模型分別實現了 15.2% 和 2.2% 的提升。<lb/>在各種模型中一致的性能增益顯示了 PiCA 的強大泛化能力。<lb/>代碼可在 https://github.com/novdream/PiCA 獲得。
2605.09287.author: Dongyi Liu et.al.
2605.09287.authors: Dongyi Liu, Yifan Niu, Qinwen Wang, Han Xiao, Jia Li
